title: senecio gibbosus subsp. ambiguus
intro: Senecio gibbosus subsp. ambiguus, also known as Rough-leaved Ragwort, is a plant species within the Senecio genus, characterized by its thick, hairy leaves and small yellow flowers. It is used in herbal medicine due to its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions, and is believed to offer health benefits like reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Senecio gibbosus subsp. ambiguus is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using the plant due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
